resources.string: Type this PIN on snipit.beam on your PC — code pastes straight into your IDE
Enter your credentials to access the repository.
I\'m Snip AI — your in-app copilot for SnipIT. Paste code or ask anything about your Vault, Beam, Snap, or Dev-Dex. I answer like we\'re pair-programming here, not like a random web chat.
Delete every Snip AI session and message on this device? This cannot be undone.
Save snippets to the Vault. Each new snip counts toward this badge — no typing the same boilerplate twice.
Generate Beam PIN sessions so your PC can pull code from Firebase at snipit.beam. Each new PIN session counts.
Open Dev-Dex, tap a command for the full mini man-page, then use Copy command. Each copy increments progress.
Paste or type some code first.
Unlocked — keep using SnipIT to level up.
Use Auto-Snip at least once from a Snip AI reply to save AI output straight to your Vault.
Save snippets that came from Snap-to-Snip (OCR) to the Vault — real-world captures count.
Open SnipIT on consecutive days and do at least one action (save, beam, chat, snap). Opening the app counts toward your streak.

Your progression within the SnipIT ecosystem is measured by the quality and frequency of your platform interactions. Points are distributed based on the following algorithm:

[REAL-TIME SCAN]: +20 XP
Manual or background session OCR capture from physical documents.

[COMMIT TO VAULT]: +15 XP
Saving unique snippets or blueprints to your local secure repository.

[AI AUTO-SNIP]: +15 XP
Leveraging Snip AI for code refactoring and direct vault storage.

[BEAM SYNC]: +10 XP
Successfully synchronizing code across multi-node hardware sessions.

[DEX LOOKUP]: +2 XP
Referencing command blueprints from the Dev-Dex central repository.

SnipIT was engineered to bridge the gap between physical references and digital execution. It serves as a unified repository for high-value code blocks, command references, and development blueprints. By centralizing your knowledge base with AI-augmented tools, SnipIT accelerates your workflow from capture to deployment.\n\nCORE MISSION: To empower developers with a portable, secure, and intelligent environment that turns every piece of information into an actionable asset.

Your Dev-XP reflects your activity within the SnipIT ecosystem. Earn points by: 
• Saving code (+15 XP)
• Real-time scanning (+20 XP)
• AI-driven refactoring (+15 XP)
• Cross-device beaming (+10 XP)
• Command reference lookups (+2 XP)
All progress is securely hashed and stored on-device to track your journey from Novice to Code Deity.
Full-screen editor — monospace, scrollable. Language uses the same searchable list as New Snip.
Another device can import this from Vault → Scan QR (photo of this code) or any QR reader, then paste into SnipIT.
Add some code before generating a QR.
Code is too long for a single QR. Share a shorter snippet or split it.
Point your camera at a SnipIT QR code or pick a screenshot from your gallery.
No QR found in that image.
Remove this snippet from the Vault?
Tap a card for the full editor (IDE-style) with AI fix, or use ✨ on a row. + adds a new snippet.

Copy command — Puts this exact line on your Android clipboard. On your PC or Mac, paste it (Ctrl+V / Cmd+V) into a terminal, script, or editor.\n\nSnip to Vault — Saves the same line as a snippet in SnipIT so you can edit, Beam, or tag it later.
Tip: Add longer notes in assets/dex/*.json (see dex/README.txt) — each entry can include a big \"documentation\" field.
Open the Vault tab, open a snippet (or tap 📡 on a card), and Beam will load here. Opening Beam alone does not pick a snippet.
Ready — scan again for a new capture.
Text is read only after you take a picture
Line up your code in the frame — tap Take picture when you are ready
Frame your shot. Nothing is read until you capture.
Done — you can edit, save, or run Fix with AI.
Snip AI is cleaning up the text…
No text found in that photo — try again or move closer.
Couldn\'t take a photo. Try again.
Couldn\'t read text from the photo.
Snip AI request failed. Check your connection and OPENROUTER_API_KEY in local.properties.
